title: santolina chamaecyparissus var. squarrosa
intro: Santolina chamaecyparissus var. squarrosa, also known as the grey santolina or cotton lavender, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. squarrosa include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Santolina chamaecyparissus var. squarrosa include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
